title: Cross-Merchant Enumeration Burst with Geographic Scatter Pattern
narrative: Compromised card tested across 8-12 different merchants within 45-75 minutes, each transaction $15-$89 to stay below manual review thresholds. Fraudster systematically enumerates merchant acceptance before attempting larger frauds elsewhere. Transactions span 4+ merchant category codes (often gas stations MCC 5541, fast food MCC 5814, digital goods MCC 5816, convenience stores MCC 5499) with no geographic clusteringâ€”dist1 values exceed 500km between consecutive purchases. All transactions CNP with identical device_id but rotating P_emaildomain across free providers. Card shows zero prior transaction history (card1_txn_count_1day = 0 before burst). Transaction amounts deliberately vary by $3-$8 to appear organic. Burst terminates abruptly after 55-80 minutes, suggesting automated testing script with timeout. Card1_amt_zscore spikes to 8+ during window.
indicators: card1_txn_count_1hr >= 8 with unique MIDs each transaction
TransactionAmt range $15-$89, no amount repeated exactly
4+ distinct MCC codes within single hour window
P_emaildomain changes 3+ times within burst, all free providers
summary: A card with no recent history suddenly fires 8-12 transactions across different merchant types in under 75 minutes, with impossibly large distances between purchases and rotating email domains on each attempt.
reasoning: The fraudster is testing a stolen card's validity and spending limits across multiple merchants before selling the card data or using it for larger purchases, using geographic impossibility and email rotation to evade detection.
indicator_explanations: Card hits eight different merchants in one hour with no repeats
Amounts vary strategically to avoid triggering fixed-threshold fraud rules
Purchases jump between four unrelated business categories during the burst
Transactions appear in locations too far apart to travel between legitimately
Email address cycles through multiple free providers during the attack
Card showed zero activity for a week before suddenly bursting
Spending amounts are extremely abnormal compared to card's historical behavior
